lines: return { x: cx + r * Math.cos(rad), y: cy - r * Math.sin(rad) };
+  // Offsets a point tangentially (sideways along the circle) so a label
+  // placed near an arrow's tip never sits directly on the arrow's line,
+  // even if another arrow later points at that same angle.
+  function toTangentPoint(deg, r, sideOffset, cx = 200, cy = 200) {
+      x: cx + r * Math.cos(rad) - sideOffset * Math.sin(rad),
+      y: cy - r * Math.sin(rad) - sideOffset * Math.cos(rad)

content.text: Now let's also nudge the probe label's own offset radius slightly so it stays clear given the shorter probe shaft, and confirm the outer guide circle (r=160) still reads fine relative to the new tip radius (148).

That's fine, it sits at radius 80 along the shaft, well clear of the tip region (148) and candidate labels (radius 160, offset 26). This resolves the overlap: candidate labels are now off the exact radial line the probe travels, with generous clearance from the shortened probe arrowhead.

DONE Fixed the V7 label-overlap defect in DotAlignment.svelte: candidate labels (c1..c4) now sit at a tangentially offset position off their arrow's exact radial line (via a new toTangentPoint helper) instead of directly beyond the tip, and the probe's arrow shaft was shortened (radius 148 vs candidates' label radius 160) so the amber probe arrowhead can no longer land on top of a periwinkle candidate label at any angle, including when the probe aligns exactly with c3 at 110 degrees.

result:
That's fine, it sits at radius 80 along the shaft, well clear of the tip region (148) and candidate labels (radius 160, offset 26). This resolves the overlap: candidate labels are now off the exact radial line the probe travels, with generous clearance from the shortened probe arrowhead.

DONE Fixed the V7 label-overlap defect in DotAlignment.svelte: candidate labels (c1..c4) now sit at a tangentially offset position off their arrow's exact radial line (via a new toTangentPoint helper) instead of directly beyond the tip, and the probe's arrow shaft was shortened (radius 148 vs candidates' label radius 160) so the amber probe arrowhead can no longer land on top of a periwinkle candidate label at any angle, including when the probe aligns exactly with c3 at 110 degrees.
